message:
feat(core,block,repair): expand S3-FIFO cache workload tests, harden core lib APIs, and refine repair codec

Extends the S3-FIFO cache eviction implementation with more comprehensive
workload comparison tests that exercise promotion/demotion pathways,
ghost queue hit rates, and scan resistance under realistic access
distributions. The core library gains additional safety checks and API
surface improvements, while the repair codec receives refined
Reed-Solomon parameter handling for more robust self-healing recovery.

- Add extended cache workload comparison tests covering mixed read/write
  patterns, hot/cold partition shifts, and ghost queue admission metrics
- Expand ffs-core public API with improved type safety and documentation
  on critical data structures
- Refine ffs-repair codec Reed-Solomon block size selection and error
  envelope handling for edge cases in partial-block recovery
- Update beads issue tracker state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1c4y","title":"EPIC: S3-FIFO for ARC Buffer/Cache Eviction (Graveyard Entry 15.1)","description":"# EPIC: S3-FIFO for ARC Buffer/Cache Eviction (Graveyard Entry 15.1)

## PURPOSE
Replace the current ARC (Adaptive Replacement Cache) buffer cache eviction policy with S3-FIFO, a simpler and often superior eviction algorithm. S3-FIFO uses three FIFO queues — small (admission filter), main, and ghost — with posterior-guided adaptive queue splitting.

## BACKGROUND
From the Alien CS Graveyard entry 15.1:
- S3-FIFO (Yang et al., 2023): three-queue FIFO eviction with admission filter
- Often matches or beats ARC on filesystem workloads with simpler implementation
- No per-access metadata update (unlike ARC which adjusts T1/T2 split on every access)
- Ghost queue provides scan resistance without LRU overhead

## DESIGN

### Three Queues
1. Small Queue (S): admission filter, ~10% of total cache
   - New blocks enter here
   - Evicted from S on first access = not promoted (scan filter)
   - Evicted from S with re-access = promoted to Main
2. Main Queue (M): ~90% of total cache
   - Blocks promoted from S
   - FIFO eviction (no reordering on access)
   - Frequency counter: blocks with count > 0 get second chance
3. Ghost Queue (G): metadata-only (no data), tracks recently evicted
   - Stores block IDs of recently evicted blocks
   - Hit in ghost = block is popular, immediate promotion to Main on re-admission

### Posterior-Guided Adaptive Split
- Bayesian posterior over optimal S/M split ratio
- Update based on ghost hit rate: high ghost hits = S too small
- This extends vanilla S3-FIFO with principled adaptation (alien artifact methodology)

### Drop-In Replacement
- Same BlockCache trait interface as current ARC
- get(block_id) -> Option<BlockData>
- insert(block_id, data) -> Option<(block_id, data)> // evicted entry
- Configurability: total_capacity, small_ratio (default 0.1)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] S3-FIFO implements BlockCache trait
- [ ] Drop-in replacement for ARC (feature flag to select)
- [ ] Benchmark: hit rate comparison vs ARC on filesystem trace
- [ ] Ghost queue provides scan resistance
- [ ] Adaptive split improves over fixed 10/90 split
- [ ] Unit tests: basic FIFO eviction, promotion, ghost hits

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E cache test (bd-1c4y.3) passes
3. S3-FIFO resists sequential scan pollution
4. Hit rate improvement over plain ARC on mixed workloads
5.  {"id":"bd-1c4y.1","title":"Implement S3-FIFO eviction for ffs-block ARC cache","description":"# Implement S3-FIFO eviction for ffs-block ARC cache

## GOAL
Implement the S3-FIFO eviction algorithm as a drop-in replacement for the current ARC cache in the ffs-block crate.

## DESIGN

### Data Structures
```rust
struct S3FifoCache<K, V> {
    small: VecDeque<(K, V, u8)>,      // (key, value, access_count)
    main: VecDeque<(K, V, u8)>,       // (key, value, access_count)
    ghost: VecDeque<K>,                // key only, no data
    index: HashMap<K, QueueLocation>, // O(1) lookup
    small_capacity: usize,            // ~10% of total
    main_capacity: usize,             // ~90% of total
    ghost_capacity: usize,            // same as total
}
```

### Operations
- get(key): lookup in index, increment access_count, return data
- insert(key, value):
  1. If key in ghost: insert directly to Main (popular block returning)
  2. Else: insert to Small
  3. If Small full: evict from Small
     - If evicted had access_count > 0: promote to Main
     - Else: move to Ghost, discard data
  4. If Main full: evict from Main
     - If evicted had access_count > 0: re-insert at tail (second chance), decrement count
     - Else: discard

### Integration
- Feature flag: --features s3fifo to select S3-FIFO over ARC
- Same BlockCache trait interface
- Location: ffs-block/src/cache/s3fifo.rs

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Implements BlockCache trait identically to ARC
- [ ] Small queue filters one-hit-wonder scans
- [ ] Main queue provides frequency-based retention
- [ ] Ghost queue enables fast re-admission of popular evicted blocks
- [ ] Feature flag selects S3-FIFO vs ARC at compile time
- [ ] Unit tests: insert, get, eviction order, ghost hit promotion
- [ ] Property tests: cache never exceeds capacity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IronPeak","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:29.966282698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T22:34:21.745197242Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T22:34:21.745130367Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","eviction","s3fifo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1c4y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:29.966282698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":87,"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section. Every implementation bead must specify structured log events at trace/debug/info/warn/error levels with field names. Add logging spec before implementation begins.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:19Z"},{"id":112,"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.1","author":"MossyKnoll","text":"Logging requirements for this bead (added before implementation): TRACE target=ffs::block::s3fifo for queue transitions (small/main/ghost), promotions, second-chance rotations, and victim selection with fields block, from_queue, to_queue, access_count, small_len, main_len, ghost_len. DEBUG target=ffs::block::s3fifo for admission decisions and ghost-hit re-admission with fields block, reason, policy_state, capacity_state. INFO target=ffs::block::s3fifo for periodic cache summaries and mode selection (arc vs s3fifo) with fields hits, misses, evictions, ghost_hits, occupancy. WARN target=ffs::block::s3fifo for invariant pressure events (queue overflow recovery, forced drops) with fields block, queue, overflow_by. ERROR target=ffs::block::s3fifo for invariant violations that trigger hard failure with fields block, queue, detail.","created_at":"2026-02-13T21:34:06Z"},{"id":177,"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-1c4y.2","title":"Benchmark: S3-FIFO vs ARC hit rates on filesystem workloads","description":"# Benchmark: S3-FIFO vs ARC hit rates on filesystem workloads

## GOAL
Compare S3-FIFO and ARC cache hit rates on representative filesystem workloads to validate the replacement.

## WORKLOADS
1. Sequential scan: read 10000 blocks sequentially (S3-FIFO should excel — scan resistant)
2. Zipf distribution: random block access with Zipf popularity (both should perform well)
3. Mixed: 70% sequential scan + 30% random hot-key access
4. Compile workload: simulate gcc-like access pattern (many small files, some large)
5. Database workload: B-tree traversal pattern (root hot, leaves cold)

## METRICS
- Hit rate (%)
- Operations per second
- Memory overhead per cached block
- p50/p95/p99 lookup latency

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] S3-FIFO matches or beats ARC on >= 3 of 5 workloads
- [ ] Memory overhead per block is <= ARC
- [ ] Results documented with reproducible benchmark harness
- [ ] Baseline captured for regression tracking","notes":"2026-02-18 FoggyRiver: benchmark/report plumbing remains in place, but current S3FIFO policy still misses acceptance (latest direct runs remain 0 wins / 4 losses / 1 tie vs ARC on hit-rate). Attempted tuning passes (capacity split and ghost-list strategy) regressed further and were reverted. Leaving this bead open pending a stronger policy change or workload-contract decision.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralReef","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:39.580753067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T01:24:25.028705147Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T01:24:25.028627291Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","cache"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1c4y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:39.580753067Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":176,"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

-{"id":"bd-1c4y.3","title":"E2E test: S3-FIFO cache correctness and scan resistance","description":"# E2E test: S3-FIFO cache correctness and scan resistance

## GOAL
Prove that the S3-FIFO cache implementation is correct (no data loss, no stale reads) and provides scan resistance (sequential scan does not evict frequently-accessed hot data).

## TEST SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Basic Correctness
1. Insert 1000 blocks into cache (capacity 500)
2. Verify: most recently accessed 500 blocks present (modulo eviction policy)
3. Get all present blocks: verify data matches what was inserted
4. No stale data: every get returns current version or None

### Scenario 2: Scan Resistance
1. Pre-warm cache with 100 hot blocks (access each 10 times)
2. Sequential scan: read 1000 cold blocks (one-hit wonders)
3. After scan: verify hot blocks still in cache (not evicted by scan)
4. This is the key S3-FIFO advantage over LRU

### Scenario 3: Ghost Queue Re-Admission
1. Fill cache, causing eviction of popular block B
2. B enters ghost queue (metadata only)
3. Access B again: should be re-admitted directly to Main queue
4. Verify: B is in Main (not Small), will resist next eviction

### Scenario 4: Capacity Invariant
1. Run 100K random insert/get operations
2. After every operation: verify cache size <= capacity
3. Verify: no memory leak (track allocation count)

### Scenario 5: Drop-In ARC Replacement
1. Run identical workload through both S3-FIFO and ARC
2. Verify: same set of operations produces same observable behavior
   (keys present/absent may differ due to policy, but API contract identical)
3. Verify: BlockCache trait fully implemented (no panics, no unimplemented!())

### Scenario 6: Integration with Filesystem Read Path
1. Mount FrankenFS with S3-FIFO cache (feature flag)
2. Read 1000 files, verify all content correct
3. Read same 100 files repeatedly (hot set), then scan 5000 new files
4. Re-read hot 100 files: verify cache hits (scan did not evict them)
5. Check evidence ledger: cache hit/miss rates logged

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] No stale reads or data corruption from cache
- [ ] Scan resistance: hot blocks survive sequential scan
- [ ] Ghost queue correctly promotes re-accessed blocks
- [ ] Cache size never exceeds configured capacity
- [ ] Drop-in replacement for ARC (same trait, same API)
- [ ] Integration test passes with real filesystem workload
- [ ] All scenarios deterministic and CI-executable
- [ ] Logging: cache hit/miss ratio, eviction counts, promotion events","notes":"2026-02-18 FoggyRiver: Added new deterministic S3FIFO ArcCache read-path tests in crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs: s3fifo_arc_cache_read_path_preserves_written_bytes, s3fifo_arc_cache_scan_resistance_on_read_path, s3fifo_arc_cache_ghost_reaccess_promotes_to_main_queue, s3fifo_arc_cache_capacity_invariant_under_random_access. Validation: rch cargo check/clippy passed for -p ffs-block --all-targets --features s3fifo; targeted rch test run passed (4/4 new tests). Remaining for full bead closure: explicit drop-in ARC-vs-S3 API-behavior comparison harness and mount-surface integration/log assertions.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:09.621012769Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T01:08:07.134835701Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","e2e","s3fifo","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1c4y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:09.621012769Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1c4y.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:23:09.621012769Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":160,"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Priority tier: S/A

 {"id":"bd-1c4y.4","title":"Add unit tests for S3-FIFO eviction policy","description":"# Add unit tests for S3-FIFO eviction policy

## GOAL
Provide unit test coverage for the S3-FIFO epic (bd-1c4y). Currently has E2E test (bd-1c4y.3) and benchmark (bd-1c4y.2) but NO unit tests for the three-queue eviction logic, scan resistance, or promotion/demotion paths.

## TEST PLAN

### Queue Structure Tests
1. New entry goes to small queue (S)
2. Second access promotes from S to main queue (M)
3. Eviction from S goes to ghost queue (G)
4. Re-access from G promotes to M (scan resistance)
5. Queue size ratios maintained (S=10%, M=90%)

### Eviction Tests
6. Eviction selects from S first (FIFO order)
7. S empty: eviction falls through to M (FIFO order)
8. Ghost queue bounded (does not grow unbounded)
9. Cache at capacity: insert triggers eviction

### Scan Resistance Tests
10. Sequential scan does not pollute M queue
11. Hot entries survive sequential scan
12. Mixed workload: hot set retained, scan set evicted

### Integration with ARC
13. S3-FIFO can replace ARC eviction policy
14. Cache hit/miss metrics correct under S3-FIFO

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
All 14 tests above. Deterministic.

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Test failures log queue sizes and contents

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 14 tests pass
2. Scan resistance verified (hot entries retained)
3. No unbounded memory growth","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IronPeak","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:17.235905478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T22:35:00.513428251Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cache","s3fifo","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1c4y","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:02:17.235905478Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1c4y.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:03:50.663798987Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":157,"issue_id":"bd-1c4y.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-2r8","title":"Implement adaptive backpressure system with budget propagation","description":"# Implement adaptive backpressure system with budget propagation

## GOAL
Implement Cx-based budget system that propagates backpressure through the stack, preventing cascade failures under load.

## BACKGROUND (Alien-Artifact Quality)
Traditional systems fail abruptly under load. FrankenFS must degrade gracefully using information-theoretic principles:

1. **Budget = scarce resource allocation**
   - deadline: wall-clock timeout
   - poll_quota: async poll iterations allowed
   - cost_quota: abstract "work units" allowed
   - priority: relative importance

2. **Budget combination**: min for limits, max for priority

3. **Backpressure = signal to reduce demand**
   When budget exhausted, callers must either:
   - Retry with fresh budget (if transient)
   - Propagate failure (if permanent)
   - Shed load (if graceful degradation enabled)

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Budget Types
```rust
pub struct Budget {
    pub deadline: Option<Instant>,
    pub poll_quota: Option<u64>,
    pub cost_quota: Option<u64>,
    pub priority: Priority,
}

impl Budget {
    pub fn combine(&self, other: &Budget) -> Budget {
        Budget {
            deadline: min_option(self.deadline, other.deadline),
            poll_quota: min_option(self.poll_quota, other.poll_quota),
            cost_quota: min_option(self.cost_quota, other.cost_quota),
            priority: max(self.priority, other.priority),
        }
    }

    pub fn is_exhausted(&self) -> bool {
        self.deadline.map_or(false, |d| Instant::now() > d)
            || self.poll_quota == Some(0)
            || self.cost_quota == Some(0)
    }
}
```

### 2. Integration Points
- **FUSE dispatch**: Per-request budget from config
- **ARC cache**: Eviction budget (prevent eviction storms)
- **MVCC commit**: Transaction budget
- **Scrub/GC**: Background budget (yields to foreground)
- **RaptorQ decode**: Symbol budget

### 3. Pressure Detection
```rust
pub struct PressureMonitor {
    pub fn sample_cpu_load() -> f64;
    pub fn sample_memory_pressure() -> f64;
    pub fn sample_io_depth() -> usize;
    pub fn compute_degradation_level() -> DegradationLevel;
}
```

### 4. Graceful Degradation FSM
```
Normal -> Pause Background -> Reduce Cache -> Throttle Writes -> Read-Only
   ^                                                              |
   +--------------------(pressure relieved)----------------------+
```

## TESTS
1. Unit: Budget combination semantics
2. Unit: Pressure detection thresholds
3. Integration: Simulated CPU load triggers degradation
4. Integration: Recovery when pressure relieved

## LOGGING
- Budget exhaustion (warn)
- Degradation level change (info)
- Pressure samples (trace)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Operations respect budget limits
2. Degradation level reflects actual pressure
3. Recovery is automatic and stable
4. No infinite loops or deadlocks under pressure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:51:09.209435721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T20:27:30.741671654Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T20:27:30.741640536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","perf","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.370992350Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-3tz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:39.081031452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-3tz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.977623160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2s4","title":"EPIC: Production FUSE Runtime","description":"# EPIC: Production FUSE Runtime

## PURPOSE
Transform the FUSE interface from scaffold to production-ready mount runtime, as required by FEATURE_PARITY.md blocking gap #3.

## BACKGROUND
From spec §14 (FUSE Integration):
- Multi-threaded dispatch required
- All state must be Send + Sync
- Never block FUSE dispatch thread on raw I/O
- Proper lifecycle management (mount/unmount/signal handling)

Current state: "FUSE mount runtime: ❌ Interface scaffold only"

## CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Mount Lifecycle
- Clean mount with explicit options (ro/rw, cache_size, etc.)
- Graceful unmount with flush and cleanup
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT -> clean unmount)
- Crash recovery on restart

### 2. Multi-threaded Dispatch
- Use fuser multi-threaded mode
- Thread pool sizing based on CPU count
- Per-thread ARC cache access (Send + Sync)
- Connection pooling for concurrent requests

### 3. Error Handling
- Every FUSE op maps to correct errno
- Timeout handling for hung operations
- Partial failure recovery

### 4. Performance
- Request batching where applicable
- Readahead for sequential access
- Writeback coalescing
- Attribute caching with configurable TTL

### 5. Observability
- Per-op latency metrics
- Request queue depth
- Error rate tracking

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount --rw /path/to/image.ext4 /mnt/ffs` works in production
2. Concurrent readers/writers do not deadlock
3. `umount /mnt/ffs` is clean (no data loss)
4. SIGTERM results in clean shutdown
5. All FUSE ops have correct errno mapping

## FILES
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs (main implementation)
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/mount.rs (lifecycle)
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/dispatch.rs (multi-thread)
- crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs (mount command)

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E suite (bd-3en1) passes
3. ffs mount --rw works on ext4 and btrfs images
4. SIGTERM unmount is clean (no data loss)
5. All FUSE ops have correct errno mapping","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:01.826764621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:27:35.868189807Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","fuse","reliability"],"comments":[{"id":91,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section.  {"id":"bd-2s4.1","title":"Implement FUSE write operations dispatch (create/mkdir/write/unlink/rename)","description":"# Implement FUSE write operations dispatch

## GOAL
Add write operation handlers to FrankenFuse so that mounted filesystems support mutation operations.

## BACKGROUND
Currently ffs-fuse only handles read operations (getattr, lookup, readdir, read, readlink). Write operations need:
1. FUSE protocol handlers in FrankenFuse
2. Delegation to FsOps trait methods
3. Transaction wrapping (begin → operation → commit)
4. Error mapping for write-specific errors (ENOSPC, EROFS, EEXIST, etc.)

## DELIVERABLES
### Phase 1: File creation and writing
1. `create(parent, name, mode, umask, flags)` → create file + open
2. `write(ino, fh, offset, data, flags)` → write data to file
3. `setattr(ino, mode, uid, gid, size, atime, mtime)` → chmod/chown/truncate

### Phase 2: Directory operations  
4. `mkdir(parent, name, mode, umask)` → create directory
5. `unlink(parent, name)` → remove file
6. `rmdir(parent, name)` → remove directory
7. `rename(parent, name, newparent, newname, flags)` → move/rename

### Phase 3: Link operations
8. `link(ino, newparent, newname)` → hard link
9. `symlink(parent, name, link)` → symbolic link
10. `mknod(parent, name, mode, rdev)` → device/fifo/socket

### Each handler must:
- Check read-only mode → EROFS
- Begin MVCC transaction
- Delegate to FsOps implementation
- Commit or abort transaction
- Map errors to errno
- Log operation at appropriate level

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: create + write + read roundtrip
2. Unit: mkdir + create file inside + readdir
3. Unit: unlink reduces link count
4. Unit: rmdir on non-empty → ENOTEMPTY
5. Unit: rename across directories
6. Unit: EROFS on read-only mount
7. Unit: ENOSPC when disk full (simulated)
8. Integration: 100 concurrent file creates
9. Property: Random create/delete sequences maintain consistency

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
### Operation-level logging (all at debug unless error)
- create (debug): parent_ino, name, mode, result_ino
- write (trace): ino, offset, len, written_bytes (not data content)
- setattr (debug): ino, changed_fields (mode/size/time), old_values, new_values
- mkdir (debug): parent_ino, name, mode, result_ino
- unlink (debug): parent_ino, name, target_ino, remaining_links
- rmdir (debug): parent_ino, name, target_ino
- rename (debug): old_parent, old_name, new_parent, new_name, flags

### Transaction logging
- txn_begin (trace): txn_id, operation, inode(s)
- txn_commit (trace): txn_id, commit_seq, duration_us
- txn_abort (debug): txn_id, reason

### Error logging
- write_error (warn): operation, ino, errno, error_context
- permission_denied (info): operation, ino, uid, required_mode
- disk_full (warn): operation, requested_blocks, available_blocks

### Performance logging
- slow_operation (info): operation, ino, duration_ms (if > 100ms)

## POSIX COMPLIANCE NOTES
- create: EEXIST if exists and O_EXCL, ENOSPC if disk full
- mkdir: EEXIST, EACCES for permission
- unlink: EISDIR (use rmdir instead), ENOENT
- rmdir: ENOTEMPTY, ENOTDIR
- rename: atomic replace semantics (POSIX.1-2017)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 10 FUSE write operations implemented
2. Transaction wrapping correct (commit on success, abort on error)
3. Read-only mount correctly rejects writes with EROFS
4. Errno mapping matches POSIX expectations
5. All operations logged with sufficient context for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:34.624724548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:18:08.152211645Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:18:08.152137927Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2ad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:01.857993659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:34.624724548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-huh.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:01.951296010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":44,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"POSIX COMPLIANCE: Each write operation must handle specific error cases: create → EEXIST if exists, ENOSPC if disk full; mkdir → EEXIST, EACCES; unlink → EISDIR (use rmdir instead), ENOENT; rmdir → ENOTEMPTY, ENOTDIR; rename → atomic replace semantics. Reference POSIX.1-2017 for exact behavior.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2s4.2","title":"Implement FUSE setxattr and removexattr operations","description":"# Implement FUSE setxattr and removexattr operations

## GOAL
Enable writing and removing extended attributes through the FUSE mount interface.

## BACKGROUND
listxattr and getxattr are already implemented (read-only). This bead adds the write side:
- setxattr: Create or replace an extended attribute
- removexattr: Delete an extended attribute

Requires inline xattr space management (may need to promote to external block if inline space exhausted).

## DELIVERABLES
1. FrankenFuse::setxattr() handler
2. FrankenFuse::removexattr() handler
3. Inline xattr space management (check if fits, promote if needed)
4. Integration with MVCC transaction wrapping

## TESTS
1. Set xattr on file, read it back
2. Replace existing xattr with larger value
3. Remove xattr, verify gone
4. Overflow inline space → external block (or ENOSPC if not supported)
5. EROFS on read-only mount

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. POSIX xattr semantics preserved
2. Transaction-wrapped for crash safety
3. No data corruption on inline/external boundary","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.295902137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:11:54.424340962Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T20:11:54.424320825Z","close_reason":"Implemented FUSE setxattr/removexattr with core ext4 xattr mutation wiring, ENOSPC fallback, RO semantics, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.295902137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.398307753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-2s4.3","title":"Implement crash recovery on mount after unclean shutdown","description":"# Implement crash recovery on mount after unclean shutdown

## GOAL
When FrankenFS mounts a filesystem image that was not cleanly unmounted (crash, SIGKILL, power loss), it must detect the unclean state and perform recovery before serving any requests.

## BACKGROUND
Unclean shutdown can leave:
- Uncommitted MVCC transactions (partial writes in version chains)
- Stale RaptorQ symbols (dirty groups not refreshed)
- Incomplete journal transactions (JBD2 write path)
- Dirty inode/block caches not flushed to disk

Without crash recovery, mounting after a crash could expose partially-written data, stale versions, or inconsistent metadata.

## DESIGN

### Unclean Shutdown Detection
- Superblock dirty flag: set on mount, cleared on clean unmount
- If dirty flag is set on mount: unclean shutdown detected
- Journal recovery: check for uncommitted journal transactions

### Recovery Steps (ordered)
1. Journal replay: apply committed but unflushed journal transactions
2. MVCC rollback: abort any in-flight transactions (no commit record = abort)
3. Version chain cleanup: remove uncommitted versions from chains
4. RaptorQ symbol validation: mark all groups as needing re-verification
5. Trigger immediate scrub of metadata-critical groups (superblock, GDT, inode tables)
6. Clear superblock dirty flag after recovery completes
7. Log all recovery actions to evidence ledger

### Integration Points
- ffs-cli mount command: detect unclean state before serving FUSE
- Journal replay (bd-1xe.4): already handles ext4 journal replay
- MVCC subsystem: needs abort/rollback API for in-flight transactions
- Scrub daemon: needs priority queue for post-recovery metadata verification

### Error Handling
- If recovery fails (journal corrupt, metadata unreadable): refuse to mount, log detailed error
- If recovery partial (some transactions recovered, others not): mount read-only, log warning
- Never silently ignore unclean state

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Unclean shutdown detected via superblock dirty flag
- [ ] Journal transactions replayed correctly on recovery mount
- [ ] In-flight MVCC transactions aborted cleanly
- [ ] RaptorQ symbols re-verified after crash recovery
- [ ] Evidence ledger records all recovery actions
- [ ] Mount refuses if recovery fails completely
- [ ] Read-only fallback if recovery is partial
- [ ] Unit tests: mock unclean state, verify recovery steps","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:08.758264993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:32:45.652294709Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:32:45.652258782Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crash","fuse","recovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1xe.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:25.889600663Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:09.698886837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2ad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:08.636193258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:08.758264993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":80,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section. Every implementation bead must specify structured log events at trace/debug/info/warn/error levels with field names. Add logging spec before implementation begins.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:18Z"}]}
